content.text: What is C++23?In February of 2023, the ISO (International Organization for Standardization) approved a new version of C++, called C++23.New improvements in C++23For your interest, here’s a list of the major changes that C++23 adds. Note that this list is not comprehensive, but rather intended to highlight some of the key changes of interest.Constexpr <cmath> (e.g.std::abs()), and <cstdlib> (6.7 -- Relational operators and floating point comparisons).Constexprstd::unique_ptr(no lesson yet).Explicitthisparameter (no lesson yet).Fixed-size floating point types (via <stdfloat>) (no lesson yet).Formatted printing functionsstd::printandstd::println(no lesson yet)Literal suffixes forstd::size_tand the corresponding signed type (5.2 -- Literals).Multidimensional subscriptoperator[](Mentioned in lesson17.13 -- Multidimensional std::array).Multidimensional spanstd::mdspan(17.13 -- Multidimensional std::array).Preprocessor directives#elifdefand#elifndef(no lesson yet).Preprocessor directive#warning(no lesson yet).Stacktrace library (no lesson yet)Standard library modulesstd(andstd.compat) (no lesson yet).Staticoperator()andoperator[](no lesson yet).std::bitsetnow fully constexpr.std::expected(no lesson yet)std::rangesalgorithmsstarts_with,ends_with,contains(no lesson yet)std::string::containsandstd::string_view::contains(no lesson yet)std::to_underlyingto get the underlying type of enum (13.6 -- Scoped enumerations (enum classes)).std::unreachable()(no lesson yet).Using unknown pointers and references in constant expressions (17.2 -- std::array length and indexing).Next lessonC.1The end?Back to table of contentsPrevious lessonB.4Introduction to C++20

In February of 2023, the ISO (International Organization for Standardization) approved a new version of C++, called C++23.New improvements in C++23For your interest, here’s a list of the major changes that C++23 adds.
